description: Executable evals for the sdr skill. Verifies the four-type routing surface and that the skill points at the canonical template path rather than inlining template content.
_contract_note: The skill points at ~/repos/system-design-records/ as the canonical template source. That path is an external dependency NOT owned by this repo — drift in the upstream repo (renamed templates, restructured layout) will surface as eval drift here. Re-pin the regex if the upstream template filenames change. Per ADR #0005, structural assertions (skill_invoked + tool_input_matches) are the load-bearing signal; text regex is diagnostic-tier in spirit. EXCEPTION: the multi-turn evals below permanently classify chain_order and skill_invoked_in_turn as diagnostic-tier per the 2026-04-21 substrate-path decision documented in skills/systems-analysis/evals/evals.json sunk-cost-migration-multi-turn — `claude --resume` does not reliably re-emit Skill tool_use across resumed turns, so per-turn structural signals on turns 2..N cannot gate. Required-tier signal in those evals is the turn-1 DTP `skill_invoked` structural gate (which IS reliable on fresh-session) plus turn-2 template-name regex on prose. The `[Stage: Problem Definition]` text marker has been demoted to diagnostic-tier across Class A turn-1 assertions per observed flakiness — DTP fires structurally but does not always emit the literal stage marker text. ANTI-TRIGGER ASYMMETRY: only does-not-fire-for-tool-adoption is multi-turn; does-not-fire-for-single-architectural-choice and does-not-fire-for-tenet-deviation remain single-turn. Rationale: the latter two prompts contain enough problem-shape signal that DTP fast-tracks rather than intercepting (they name the deviation/decision concretely), so the routing decision happens in a single pass. The tool-adoption prompt is bare ('Should we adopt X?') with no stated problem, so DTP intercepts and the actual routing decision happens on turn 2 after problem clarification. SLASH-BYPASS BUG: issue #157 documents that /sdr <bare-prompt> short-circuits the DTP HARD-GATE — model prose-routes without invoking Skill tool. The Class A multi-turn turn-1 prompts here have been written WITHOUT the /sdr prefix to test the DTP-intercept path correctly; do NOT add the prefix back without first resolving #157.

evals.name: fires-on-create-sdr-trigger
evals.summary: skill fires on /sdr-style request and surfaces the four-type routing — Class B in-place demotion: skill_invoked / tool_input_matches demoted to diagnostic per observed Skill re-emit unreliability on slash-prefix prompts (model writes prose response without re-invoking Skill tool); regex remains the load-bearing required signal.
evals.prompt: /sdr — I want to write a system design record for a new payments platform.

assertions.description: diagnostic: skill_invoked channel — Skill tool re-emit on slash-prefix prompts is unreliable per issue #157 and the Class B observed pattern; informational
diagnostic: Skill tool surface contract — same re-emit unreliability as above
required: skill output references at least one of the four canonical SDR types — load-bearing text signal per ADR #0005 channel-reliability framing
required: skill engages with SDR concepts rather than answering generically.

evals.name: routes-to-system-overview-for-platform-design
evals.summary: Class A multi-turn rewrite: turn 1 bare-platform prompt (no problem statement, no slash prefix per issue #157) → DTP HARD-GATE intercepts; turn 2 user provides problem + stakes + scaffold ask without naming the template type → SDR routes to System Overview. Required-tier gates: turn 1 DTP fires (structural) + DTP stage marker (text); turn 2 template-name regex (text). chain_order / skill_invoked_in_turn diagnostic per --resume re-emit unreliability.

turns.prompt: I'm designing a whole new order fulfillment platform across multiple services. Which template should I use?

assertions.description: Turn 1: DTP front-door fires per rules/planning.md HARD-GATE — no problem statement in prompt, so DTP must intercept before any SDR routing. Slash prefix (/sdr) deliberately omitted per issue #157.

Diagnostic: DTP stage marker emitted per planning.md Stage Visibility contract. Demoted from required to diagnostic per observed turn-1 flakiness — model fires DTP (skill_invoked passes structural gate) but does not always emit the literal `[Stage: Problem Definition]` text marker. Required structural channel above is the load-bearing gate; this is informational confirmation.

turns.prompt: Problem: our order fulfillment platform is split across 6 services with no shared landscape doc — new hires take ~2 weeks to onboard, and oncall struggles to triage cross-service incidents. Stakes: every new engineer adds ~80 hours of ramp time. Scaffold the SDR for this order fulfillment platform — I want a system overview covering all 6 services and their boundaries.

assertions.description: Turn 2: skill recommends the System Overview template by name.

Diagnostic cross-type confusion catch: response does NOT recommend Service/Component, Data Design, or Blueprint as the routing target. Diagnostic-tier because model may legitimately reference other templates while explaining the routing decision; surfaces cross-type confusion regression in transcripts without gating on prose nuance.

final_assertions.description: Diagnostic: ideal chain order DTP → sdr. Acceptable degenerate shape per --resume re-emit unreliability: [define-the-problem, (none)] when SDR doesn't re-emit on turn 2. Channel is informational; absence does not gate the eval.
Diagnostic: turn 2 SDR fires when Skill re-emits across --resume. Re-emit is unreliable; informational only.

evals.name: does-not-fire-for-single-architectural-choice
evals.summary: /adr-shaped prompt should NOT trigger sdr — anti-trigger guardrail (paired with positive /adr signal to avoid silent-fire on empty tool stream). Single-turn (not Class A multi-turn) per the anti-trigger asymmetry rationale in _contract_note: the prompt names a concrete decision so DTP fast-tracks rather than intercepting.
evals.prompt: I need to record a single architectural decision: we're choosing Postgres over MySQL for the user service. What should I do?

assertions.description: positive anchor — response recommends /adr (rules out empty-stream silent-fire)
sdr does NOT fire on a single-decision /adr-shaped request (diagnostic — silent-fires on zero tool uses; paired with positive regex above)

evals.name: routes-to-service-component-for-new-service
evals.summary: Class A multi-turn rewrite: turn 1 bare service-creation prompt → DTP HARD-GATE intercepts; turn 2 problem + stakes + scaffold ask (deliberately does NOT name the template type) → SDR routes to Service/Component Creation. Required-tier gates: turn 1 DTP structural + stage marker; turn 2 template-name regex. chain_order / skill_invoked_in_turn diagnostic.

turns.prompt: I'm building a new pricing service

assertions.description: Turn 1: DTP front-door fires per rules/planning.md HARD-GATE — bare 'building X' is greenfield-no-problem-stated, DTP intercepts
Diagnostic: DTP stage marker emitted. Demoted per observed turn-1 flakiness — see system-overview eval description for rationale.

turns.prompt: Problem: promo discounts are computed in 4 different services and drift in subtle ways depending on which service is hit first. Stakes: ~3% revenue leakage last quarter from inconsistent discount logic, plus 2 customer-facing incidents. Scaffold the SDR for the new pricing service — this is a single service/component creation, not a whole platform.

assertions.description: Turn 2: skill recommends Service/Component Creation template (matches 'Service/Component' prose with up to 20 chars between tokens, plus 'service-component' file ref).
Diagnostic cross-type confusion catch: response does NOT recommend System Overview, Data Design, or Blueprint as the routing target. Diagnostic-tier — see system-overview eval for rationale.

final_assertions.description: Diagnostic: ideal chain order DTP → sdr. Acceptable degenerate shape: [define-the-problem, (none)] when SDR doesn't re-emit on turn 2. Informational, does not gate.
Diagnostic: turn 2 SDR fire — informational per --resume re-emit unreliability.

evals.name: routes-to-data-design-for-schema
evals.summary: Class A multi-turn rewrite: turn 1 bare schema-design prompt → DTP intercepts; turn 2 problem + stakes + scaffold ask (does NOT echo template name) → SDR routes to Data Design. Required-tier gates: turn 1 DTP structural + stage marker; turn 2 template-name regex. chain_order / skill_invoked_in_turn diagnostic.

turns.prompt: I need to design the order schema

assertions.description: Turn 1: DTP front-door fires per rules/planning.md HARD-GATE — no problem statement, DTP intercepts
Diagnostic: DTP stage marker emitted. Demoted per observed turn-1 flakiness — see system-overview eval description for rationale.

turns.prompt: Problem: order data lives in 3 different tables with overlapping columns and no canonical access-pattern documentation. Stakes: every new feature triggers a 2-day modeling debate, and we've had 2 production incidents this quarter from picking the wrong table. Scaffold the SDR for the order schema design — I need an access-pattern-first data design.

assertions.description: Turn 2: skill recommends Data Design template (matches 'Data Design' prose, 'data-design' file ref, and three schema-X bigrams).
Diagnostic cross-type confusion catch: response does NOT recommend System Overview, Service/Component, or Blueprint. Diagnostic-tier — see system-overview eval for rationale.

final_assertions.description: Diagnostic: ideal chain order DTP → sdr. Acceptable degenerate shape: [define-the-problem, (none)] when SDR doesn't re-emit. Informational.
Diagnostic: turn 2 SDR fire — informational per --resume re-emit unreliability.

evals.name: routes-to-blueprint-for-reusable-pattern
evals.summary: Class A multi-turn rewrite: turn 1 bare reusable-pattern prompt → DTP intercepts; turn 2 problem + stakes + scaffold ask (does NOT echo 'Blueprint') → SDR routes to Blueprint. Required-tier gates: turn 1 DTP structural + stage marker; turn 2 template-name regex. chain_order / skill_invoked_in_turn diagnostic.

turns.prompt: Reusable golden path for new microservices

assertions.description: Turn 1: DTP front-door fires per rules/planning.md HARD-GATE — no problem statement, DTP intercepts
Diagnostic: DTP stage marker emitted. Demoted per observed turn-1 flakiness — see system-overview eval description for rationale.

assertions.value: This is an eval test case
assertions.description: Prose-channel guard (ADR #0011 / PR #93): user-visible preamble that originally tripped #85 must NOT appear in turn 1 finalText. Required-tier — regression sentinel for PR #93's scratch-cwd fix.

assertions.value: This is an evaluation test case
assertions.description: Prose-channel guard (ADR #0011 / PR #93): variant phrase. Required-tier — added for parity with DTP and systems-analysis sentinel sets.

assertions.value: This is an eval test case
assertions.description: Thinking-channel transparency (ADR #0011): private-reasoning detection. Diagnostic-tier — accepted at current rates; on fire, log only.

Canonical context-exploration step (#192): Glob/Read/Grep called on turn 1 — closes structural-assertion gap from ADR #0011 / 2026-04-28 audit. When DTP intercepts on turn 1, its 'Check the current project' step (SKILL.md) should read README/recent commits before clarifying questions; divergent transcripts skip this when thinking infers eval framing from empty cwd. Diagnostic-tier — forward-looking gate per #192 acceptance criterion.

turns.prompt: Problem: every team rolls their own microservice scaffold; we have ~12 variants of how teams wire metrics, logging, healthchecks, and config loading. Stakes: 3-4 weeks per new service to reach production-ready, and oncall pages because monitoring conventions diverge across services. Scaffold the SDR for a reusable microservice blueprint — I want a Blueprint template with variation points and a worked example.

assertions.description: Turn 2: skill recommends Blueprint template (tight pattern: 'Blueprint' is the template name; alternation with 'reusable pattern|golden path' would risk matching prompt echo).
Diagnostic cross-type confusion catch: response does NOT recommend System Overview, Service/Component, or Data Design. Diagnostic-tier — see system-overview eval for rationale.

final_assertions.description: Diagnostic: ideal chain order DTP → sdr. Acceptable degenerate shape: [define-the-problem, (none)] when SDR doesn't re-emit. Informational.
Diagnostic: turn 2 SDR fire — informational per --resume re-emit unreliability.

scratch_decoy.README.md:
# data-platform

Internal monorepo for the data-platform team's services and shared libraries.

## Layout

- `src/` — service entrypoints and shared modules
- `docs/` — runbooks, ADRs, and operational notes
- `scripts/` — one-off operational scripts

## Local development

Use the standard team toolchain (see `docs/onboarding.md`). Most services run via the workspace task runner; check individual service READMEs for exceptions.
scratch_decoy.docs/onboarding.md:
# Onboarding

1. Clone the repo
2. Install the team toolchain (see internal wiki)
3. Run `bun install` at the workspace root
4. Pick a service from `src/` and follow its README

For questions, ping #data-platform-engineering.

evals.name: does-not-fire-for-tool-adoption
evals.summary: Class A multi-turn anti-trigger rewrite: turn 1 bare tool-adoption prompt → DTP intercepts; turn 2 user explicitly clarifies tool-comparison framing → /tech-radar mention required, sdr must NOT fire. Turn-2 negative not_regex against SDR template names is DIAGNOSTIC — required positive /tech-radar regex carries the gate, and required-tier negative on a degenerate empty turn-2 would silent-fire cosmetically without adding gate signal. Diagnostic skill_invoked_in_turn: 2 / sdr in final_assertions catches the regression when re-emit happens.

turns.prompt: Should we adopt Temporal for workflow orchestration?

assertions.description: Turn 1: DTP front-door fires per rules/planning.md HARD-GATE — no problem statement, DTP intercepts before any routing decision
Diagnostic: DTP stage marker emitted. Demoted per observed turn-1 flakiness — see system-overview eval description for rationale.

turns.prompt: To clarify: I'm not designing a new system. I'm running a tool-adoption evaluation — comparing Temporal against Cadence and AWS Step Functions. The problem is we have 3 ad-hoc workflow systems today and want to consolidate on one tool. Route this to /tech-radar — that's the right skill for tool comparison, not the SDR skill.

assertions.description: Turn 2: response recommends /tech-radar or names the technology radar — explicit tool-comparison framing routes to tech-radar, not sdr. Required positive carries the eval gate.

Diagnostic: response does NOT recommend any of the four SDR template types. Demoted from required because (a) on empty turn-2 the required positive regex above already gates red (no silent-fire vector matters), and (b) the model may legitimately reference SDR template names while EXPLAINING the anti-trigger ('this is not a System Overview situation') and that thoroughness shouldn't fail the eval.

final_assertions.description: Diagnostic: ideal chain order DTP → tech-radar. Acceptable degenerate shapes: [define-the-problem] alone (no tech-radar re-emit) or [define-the-problem, tech-radar]. Informational; does not gate.
Diagnostic: turn 2 tech-radar fire when Skill re-emits across --resume. Informational.

Diagnostic anti-trigger: sdr must NOT fire on turn 2. Diagnostic per --resume re-emit unreliability — when re-emit DOES happen and shows sdr, this catches the regression. When re-emit doesn't happen at all, channel is informational.

evals.name: does-not-fire-for-tenet-deviation
evals.summary: tenet-deviation prompt routes to /tenet-exception, not sdr — anti-trigger guardrail paired with positive anchor. Single-turn (not Class A multi-turn) per the anti-trigger asymmetry rationale in _contract_note: the prompt names a concrete tenet to deviate from so DTP fast-tracks rather than intercepting.
evals.prompt: We need to deviate from the 'all services emit OpenTelemetry' tenet

assertions.description: positive anchor — response recommends /tenet-exception (rules out empty-stream silent-fire); tight pattern symmetric with /adr and /tech-radar anti-triggers
sdr does NOT fire on tenet-deviation framing (diagnostic — paired with positive regex above)

evals.name: fires-on-plain-english-trigger
evals.summary: plain-English (non-slash-command) trigger fires the skill — confirms description-driven surface. Single-turn with required-tier skill_invoked: per fires-on-create-sdr-trigger summary, re-emit unreliability is slash-prefix-specific; plain-English fresh-session invocations re-emit reliably.
evals.prompt: I want to create a system design record for our new payments platform

assertions.description: required: skill fires on plain-English trigger phrase (no slash command). Reliable per fresh single-session + non-slash-prefix; re-emit unreliability is slash-prefix-specific (see fires-on-create-sdr-trigger summary).
required: Skill surface contract — same fresh-session-plain-English reliability as above

evals.name: arg-passing-routes-to-named-template
evals.summary: /sdr <type> <title> form routes directly to the named template, no disambiguation. Single-turn with required-tier skill_invoked retained: arg-passing IS a slash-prefix prompt, but the explicit `<type>` arg gives the model a concrete routing target so the Skill re-emit pattern differs from bare /sdr invocations. If this eval starts failing on the structural channel, demote to diagnostic and elevate the regex per Class B rationale.

assertions.description: required: skill fires on /sdr <type> arg-passing form. If observed unreliable, demote per Class B pattern (see fires-on-create-sdr-trigger).
required: Skill surface contract
required: skill routes to named template (Blueprint) without re-prompting for type

required negative anchor — proves skill does NOT ask disambiguation when type is already specified in args (would be a regression: ignoring the explicit arg). Paired with required positive blueprint regex above; on empty terminal output positive fails red, so negative silent-fire is cosmetic noise rather than gate corruption.

evals.name: interactive-mode-asks-which-type
evals.summary: /sdr with no args triggers interactive disambiguation across the four types — must NOT scaffold during the ask. Class B in-place demotion: skill_invoked demoted to diagnostic per Skill re-emit unreliability on slash-prefix prompts. Negative scaffold-check changed from not_tool_input_matches (silent-fired on the correct-behavior path: asks-and-no-tools) to not_regex against scaffolded SDR-template headers — this still catches 'asks AND scaffolds' regression but no longer silent-fires when zero tools are used.

assertions.description: diagnostic: skill_invoked channel — Skill tool re-emit on slash-prefix prompts is unreliable; informational
required: skill asks a disambiguation question rather than scaffolding silently — load-bearing positive signal

required: response does NOT contain a scaffolded markdown header for any of the four SDR template types — proves skill is asking, not scaffolding. Replaces prior not_tool_input_matches (Write/sdr) which silent-fired on the correct-behavior path (asks-and-no-tools). Empty-signal vector for not_regex is terminalState==='empty', so on degenerate empty turn the required positive regex above already gates red and the not_regex silent-fire is cosmetic.

evals.name: read-hook-fires-on-conflict
evals.summary: Phase B read-on-end-of-skill hook fires when an artifact term hits an _Avoid_ alias of a CONTEXT.md canonical. Surfaces advisory line; NEVER substitutes silently. Required-tier regex pinned to the bolded advisory-pair shape (per CALLER-HOOKS.md § sdr advisory format) per ADR #0019 (discriminating-signal). Tightened post-PR-review: prior loose regex matched any prose containing both terms within 80 chars (false-positive risk).
evals.prompt: I just finished drafting the System Overview SDR body for our payments platform. The draft refers to 'Account' throughout. Run the end-of-skill glossary hooks per skills/sdr/SKILL.md step 7 — check ./CONTEXT.md and surface any conflicts before I hand off.

assertions.description: required: read hook emits the bolded advisory pair per CALLER-HOOKS.md format — Account (artifact, _Avoid_) and Customer (canonical) in **bold** markdown within close proximity. Pinned to the contract advisory shape rather than loose prose co-occurrence (issue raised in PR #406 ruflo review).
diagnostic: response uses hook-contract vocabulary (advisory / conflict / canonical / _Avoid_)
required negative: skill does NOT propose silent substitution — memory-discipline echo (CONTEXT.md is candidate, not authority; surface, never override)

scratch_decoy.CONTEXT.md:
# payments-platform

Project canonical terminology.

## Language

**Customer**
The end user of the payments service.
_Avoid_: Account, User

evals.name: read-hook-silent-on-match
evals.summary: Phase B read hook is silent when all artifact terms are canonical (no _Avoid_ matches), but write-offer still engages on a new term not in CONTEXT.md. Required-tier proves write-offer engages on the new term (PriceQuoteWorker); required negative proves read hook does NOT surface a false advisory pair. Updated post-PR-review (PR #406 ruflo): prior version required verbal read-hook signal SKILL.md doesn't mandate, false-failing on truly-silent-correct paths.
evals.prompt: I just finished drafting the Service/Component SDR body for the pricing service. The draft uses 'Customer' throughout (canonical per ./CONTEXT.md) and also introduces a new component called PriceQuoteWorker that is NOT yet in ./CONTEXT.md. Run the end-of-skill glossary hooks per skills/sdr/SKILL.md step 7 and report what each hook found.

assertions.description: required: write-offer engages on the new term PriceQuoteWorker (names the term, invokes /glossary, or names the hook flag) — proves the skill reached the write-offer stage after the silent read hook

required negative: read hook does NOT emit the bolded advisory pair when all artifact terms are canonical — pinned to the same advisory shape as the conflict eval so absence is structurally discriminable

evals.name: read-hook-silent-on-absent-context
evals.summary: Phase B read hook silent no-op when ./CONTEXT.md is absent. Skill proceeds to write-offer stage regardless. Required-tier positive on absent-or-proceed signal.
evals.prompt: I just finished drafting the Data Design SDR body for our order schema. There is no ./CONTEXT.md in this project. Run the end-of-skill glossary hooks per skills/sdr/SKILL.md step 7.

assertions.description: required: skill notes CONTEXT.md absent OR proceeds to write-offer stage. Load-bearing positive signal.
diagnostic: skill does not fabricate conflict from absent file

evals.name: read-hook-silent-on-malformed-context
evals.summary: Phase B read hook silent no-op + one-line diagnostic when CONTEXT.md is malformed (no Language section). NEVER substitutes. Required-tier positive on diagnostic-or-skip signal.
evals.prompt: I just finished drafting the Blueprint SDR body for our microservice template. ./CONTEXT.md exists in this project but has no `## Language` section. Run the end-of-skill glossary hooks per skills/sdr/SKILL.md step 7.

assertions.description: required: skill emits diagnostic for malformed CONTEXT.md OR explicitly skips read hook. Load-bearing positive.
diagnostic: skill does not substitute on malformed input
